sections.question: You asked if use of the lobby area is a contribution to the republican committee which is sponsoring the reception, and if so, how the contribution reported?
sections.conclusion: If Smith International was acting at the behest of a committee of a political party the use of the lobby would constitute a non-monetary contribution to the republican committee. In-kind contributions of fifty dollars or more must be reported in writing by the recipient committee.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a corporation's provision of its lobby space for a political reception constitutes a reportable contribution, and if so, how it must be reported.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The provision of the lobby space constitutes a non-monetary in-kind contribution that must be reported in writing by the recipient committee if the value is fifty dollars or more, with the fair market value estimated at the time received.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is partially illegible with OCR artifacts and missing text sections. Question and conclusion sections identified but text is fragmented. Confidence reduced due to text quality issues and incomplete regulatory citations. Core advice is discernible despite document quality.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked if use of the lobby area is a contribution to the republican committee which is sponsoring the reception, and if so, how the contribution reported?

CONCLUSION: If Smith International was acting at the behest of a committee of a political party the use of the lobby would constitute a non-monetary contribution to the republican committee. In-kind contributions of fifty dollars or more must be reported in writing by the recipient committee.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms via telephone that a corporation's provision of its lobby for a political reception is a non-monetary contribution requiring written reporting by the recipient committee if valued at fifty dollars or more.
